title: Local-first network-access boundary
summary: Worktrunk is local-first: the network is touched only when the user explicitly asked for it. The single sanctioned exception is the first `Repository::default_branch()` per repo, which may fall through to `git ls-remote` and then caches into `worktrunk.default-branch`; every later call is local. No other accessor (alias dispatch, hook context build, `wt statusline`, recovery paths, background TTL-cache refreshes) may reach the wire. This feature audits every potential egress (`git fetch`, `git ls-remote`, `gh`, `glab`, `az`, HTTP) and verifies each call site is one the user explicitly invoked. See CLAUDE.md > Network Access.

ownedFiles.reason: default_branch(): the one sanctioned ls-remote fallback and its worktrunk.default-branch cache
runs on every shell prompt: must never reach the wire

contextFiles.reason: PR/MR fetch: the legitimate user-invoked egress, must not run on background paths
Cmd: where external commands (git, gh, glab) spawn
alias dispatch must resolve locally, no fallthrough to the wire
Network Access policy and the single default_branch exception
